subject: Restore dmaap-sumulator command line invocation
commit_message:
Restore dmaap-sumulator command line invocation

The ability to run the dmaap-simulator from the command line as a Java
application was removed in an earlier review. The DMaaP simulator is
used extensively by developers for local testing. This commit restores
the ability to launch the DMaaP simulator as a Java application.

Issue-ID: POLICY-3474
Change-Id: I5ed04043308da32bc22a6255bdd890c2c0c5517b
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@est.tech>

OK, I figured it out, you rationalized all the simulators together, great!

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2
> > 
> > this simulator should not be used; it's functionality is entirely subsumed by policy-models-simulator
> 
> OK, let me check that....

you can find a sample config file in policy-docker/csit/config/sim-all.  If just using it for dmaap simulation, all you need is the dmaapProvider section and the "DMaaP simulator" portion of the restServers section.  Everything else can be deleted (it's possible that it needs the topicSinks/Sources/Servers sections - if so, just provide empty arrays, [], for those).

Patch Set 1:

> > > this simulator should not be used; it's functionality is entirely subsumed by policy-models-simulator
> > 
> > OK, let me check that....

> you can find a sample config file in policy-docker/csit/config/sim-all.  If just using it for dmaap simulation, all you need is the dmaapProvider section and the "DMaaP simulator" portion of the restServers section.  Everything else can be deleted (it's possible that it needs the topicSinks/Sources/Servers sections - if so, just provide empty arrays, [], for those).

what we could do, though, is add a default set of simParameters.json to the docker image, that just has what's needed for the dmaap portion, and add a flag to simulators.sh so that it can be launched without specifying any keystore/truststore info.  That would make it easier to use as a simulator, as-is.
